proposed_hypotheses.text: The cohort has a non-trivial baseline objective_response rate (i.e., not 0% or 100%), and treatments are common enough to support comparisons.

analyses.code: df['objective_response'].mean(); df[treatments].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Overall objective_response rate is 18.75% (n=50000). Treatment prevalences: treatment_7plus3 45.2%, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine 40.0%, treatment_midostaurin 15.1%, treatment_gilteritinib 15.1%, treatment_ivosidenib 7.9%, treatment_enasidenib 7.9%. Patients can receive 0-6 of these treatments (mean ~1.7). Sample is sufficient for subgroup work.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_7plus3 have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.

analyses.code: two-proportion z-test on objective_response by treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine
analyses.result_summary: objective_response: 21.96% on VEN/AZA (n=20020) vs 16.61% off (n=29980); difference +5.35 pct points; z-test p<1e-300.

analyses.code: two-proportion z-test on objective_response by treatment_7plus3
analyses.result_summary: objective_response: 18.89% on 7+3 (n=22580) vs 18.63% off (n=27420); difference +0.26 pct points; p=0.46. No detectable main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each targeted agent (treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib) has a non-zero main effect on objective_response in the unselected cohort.

analyses.code: two-proportion z-tests on objective_response by each targeted agent
analyses.result_summary: Main effects in the unselected cohort are null: midostaurin 18.57% vs 18.78% (p=0.66); gilteritinib 18.13% vs 18.86% (p=0.14); ivosidenib 18.82% vs 18.74% (p=0.91); enasidenib 18.58% vs 18.76% (p=0.78). None significant; targeted agents do not move overall response in the full cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with npm1_mutation have a higher objective_response rate than patients without npm1_mutation.
Patients with tp53_mutation have a lower objective_response rate than patients without tp53_mutation.
Patients with complex_karyotype have a lower objective_response rate than patients without complex_karyotype.
Patients flagged unfit_for_intensive have a lower objective_response rate than fit patients.

analyses.code: two-proportion z-test on objective_response by npm1_mutation
analyses.result_summary: objective_response: 23.46% NPM1+ (n=15014) vs 16.73% NPM1- (n=34986); diff +6.74 pct points; p<1e-300. Strongly supported.

analyses.code: two-proportion z-test on objective_response by tp53_mutation
analyses.result_summary: objective_response: 16.57% TP53+ (n=4985) vs 18.99% TP53- (n=45015); diff -2.42 pct points; p=3.2e-5. Direction supported (lower).

analyses.code: two-proportion z-test on objective_response by complex_karyotype
analyses.result_summary: objective_response: 16.81% complex (n=7462) vs 19.09% non-complex (n=42538); diff -2.29 pct points; p=3.1e-6. Direction supported (lower).

analyses.code: two-proportion z-test on objective_response by unfit_for_intensive
analyses.result_summary: objective_response: 22.10% unfit (n=20056) vs 16.51% fit (n=29944); diff +5.59 pct points; p<1e-300. Direction is OPPOSITE to the natural expectation (unfit usually do worse), suggesting confounding by treatment effect; flagged for interaction follow-up.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with flt3_itd or flt3_tkd mutations have a different objective_response rate than wild-type patients.
Patients with idh1_mutation or idh2_mutation have a different objective_response rate than wild-type patients.
Sex (sex_female) and secondary_aml are independently associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: two-proportion z-tests on objective_response by flt3_itd and flt3_tkd
analyses.result_summary: FLT3-ITD: 18.92% vs 18.71% (p=0.63). FLT3-TKD: 18.05% vs 18.79% (p=0.32). No detectable main effect for either.

analyses.code: two-proportion z-tests on objective_response by idh1_mutation and idh2_mutation
analyses.result_summary: IDH1: 19.09% vs 18.72% (p=0.58). IDH2: 19.15% vs 18.71% (p=0.46). No detectable main effect for either.

analyses.code: two-proportion z-tests on objective_response by sex_female and secondary_aml
analyses.result_summary: sex_female: 18.91% vs 18.62% (p=0.41). secondary_aml: 18.81% vs 18.73% (p=0.84). No association with response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response.
Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.

analyses.code: logistic regression objective_response ~ ecog_ps
analyses.result_summary: Coef = -0.353 per unit ECOG; OR rate 23.1% / 17.4% / 12.9% for ECOG 0/1/2; p=1.6e-90. Strongly negative as predicted.

analyses.code: logistic regression objective_response ~ age_years
analyses.result_summary: Coef = -0.00016 per year; mean age 67.98 in responders vs 67.99 in non-responders; p=0.88. No association — age is NOT predictive once other features captured.

analyses.code: logistic regression objective_response ~ blast_pct_marrow; t-test mean blast by response
analyses.result_summary: Mean blast_pct_marrow 58.5 in responders vs 60.3 in non-responders; logistic coef -0.00492; p=2.4e-16. Higher blast burden predicts lower response.

analyses.code: logistic regression objective_response ~ albumin_g_dl
analyses.result_summary: Coef +0.117 per g/dL; mean 3.82 (responders) vs 3.80 (non-responders); p=2.9e-7. Higher albumin predicts response, as expected for a nutrition/inflammation marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Markers of systemic inflammation/cachexia (crp_mg_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, wbc_k_per_ul, nlr) predict lower objective_response.

Routine organ-function labs (creatinine_mg_dl, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, ldh_u_l, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, bun_mg_dl) are individually associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests of each marker by objective_response status
analyses.result_summary: weight_loss_pct_6mo lower in responders (3.44 vs 3.95, p=1.7e-33); crp_mg_l lower in responders (5.69 vs 6.17, p=1.6e-6); wbc_k_per_ul lower in responders (19.27 vs 20.39, p=1.5e-4); nlr null (3.50 vs 3.51, p=0.92). Three of four supported in the predicted direction; nlr unrelated.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests of each lab by objective_response status
analyses.result_summary: All other organ labs are essentially null in the unselected cohort (creatinine p=0.71, bilirubin p=0.21, AST p=0.13, ALT p=0.20, LDH p=0.35, sodium p=0.62, potassium p=0.76, calcium p=0.81, hemoglobin p=0.15, alkaline phosphatase p=0.05, BUN p=0.55). They are not bulk drivers of response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is concentrated in unfit_for_intensive patients (positive interaction: VEN/AZA helps unfit patients more than fit patients).

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine * unfit_for_intensive'); also stratified 2x2 cells
analyses.result_summary: Stratified rates: fit + no VEN/AZA 16.83% (n=17937), fit + VEN/AZA 16.05% (n=12007), unfit + no VEN/AZA 16.30% (n=12043), unfit + VEN/AZA 30.81% (n=8013). Interaction coef +0.883 (logit), p=1.1e-78. VEN/AZA's effect is essentially confined to unfit patients; in fit patients it is null/slightly negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is amplified in patients with npm1_mutation (positive interaction).

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine * npm1_mutation')
analyses.result_summary: Stratified rates: NPM1- + no VEN/AZA 17.05%, NPM1- + VEN/AZA 16.10%, NPM1+ + no VEN/AZA 16.00%, NPM1+ + VEN/AZA 34.66%. Interaction coef +1.055 (logit), p=5.5e-102. VEN/AZA appears to act almost only in NPM1+ patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: tp53_mutation abrogates the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine response benefit (negative interaction).
complex_karyotype abrogates the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine response benefit (negative interaction).

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine * tp53_mutation')
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.409 (logit), p=4.7e-7. Stratified: in TP53- patients, VEN/AZA raises OR from 16.6% to 22.6%; in TP53+, VEN/AZA does essentially nothing (rates ~17%). TP53 mutation neutralizes the VEN/AZA benefit.

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine * complex_karyotype')
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.306 (logit), p=6.0e-6. Stratified: complex_karyotype-, VEN/AZA raises OR from 16.7% to 22.7%; complex_karyotype+, VEN/AZA effect drops markedly. Complex karyotype dampens the VEN/AZA benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each targeted agent shows a positive interaction with its canonical biomarker on objective_response: midostaurin x flt3_itd, gilteritinib x flt3_itd, ivosidenib x idh1_mutation, enasidenib x idh2_mutation.

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ treatment_X * biomarker_Y') for each pair
analyses.result_summary: midostaurin x flt3_itd interaction coef -0.106, p=0.19 (null). gilteritinib x flt3_itd coef +0.079, p=0.32 (null). ivosidenib x idh1_mutation coef -0.304, p=0.077 (null/wrong direction). enasidenib x idh2_mutation coef +0.039, p=0.79 (null). None of the canonical targeted-therapy biomarker interactions are present in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_7plus3 shows a positive interaction with fitness markers (effect concentrated in fit / lower-ECOG patients).

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ treatment_7plus3 * unfit_for_intensive') and logit('objective_response ~ treatment_7plus3 * ecog_ps')
analyses.result_summary: 7+3 x unfit_for_intensive interaction coef -0.027, p=0.56. 7+3 x ecog_ps coef -0.015, p=0.66. 7+3 has no effect anywhere — not in fit patients, not stratified by ECOG. It is essentially inactive in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The NPM1+ main-effect benefit on objective_response is driven entirely by NPM1+ patients who received VEN/AZA, not an intrinsic NPM1+ response advantage.

analyses.code: df.groupby(['npm1_mutation','treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine'])['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: NPM1+ patients off VEN/AZA: 16.00% OR (n=8989). NPM1- patients off VEN/AZA: 17.05% (n=25997). The NPM1 main effect (overall +6.7 pp) disappears once VEN/AZA is removed; in fact NPM1+ slightly underperform NPM1- without VEN/AZA. The 'NPM1 advantage' is entirely a treatment effect mediated by VEN/AZA.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The unfit_for_intensive main-effect benefit on objective_response is similarly driven entirely by unfit patients who received VEN/AZA, not an intrinsic unfit-patient advantage.

analyses.code: df.groupby(['unfit_for_intensive','treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine'])['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Unfit + no VEN/AZA: 16.30%. Fit + no VEN/AZA: 16.83%. With VEN/AZA removed, unfit and fit patients have nearly identical OR; the apparent unfit 'advantage' is entirely attributable to the VEN/AZA + unfit interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: VEN/AZA's effect on objective_response is greatest among unfit_for_intensive AND npm1_mutation patients (joint two-way modifier set).

analyses.code: df.groupby(['unfit_for_intensive','npm1_mutation','treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine'])['objective_response'].agg(['mean','count'])
analyses.result_summary: VEN/AZA effect by joint cell: fit/NPM1- on/off ~16% (no effect); fit/NPM1+ on/off ~16% (no effect); unfit/NPM1- on/off ~17% (essentially no effect); unfit/NPM1+ on 53.3% vs off 16.3% (n=2401 vs 3585), absolute +37 pp. Joint requirement of unfit AND NPM1+ explains the VEN/AZA signal far better than either alone.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within unfit_for_intensive AND npm1_mutation patients, VEN/AZA's effect on objective_response is further amplified when tp53_mutation is absent AND complex_karyotype is absent (i.e., these adverse-risk lesions individually neutralize the response).

analyses.code: df.groupby(['tp53_mutation','complex_karyotype','treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine'])['objective_response'].mean() restricted to unfit & NPM1+
analyses.result_summary: Within unfit+NPM1+: TP53-/complex- on VEN/AZA 78.6% vs off 15.8% (n=1790 vs 2741, +63 pp). TP53-/complex+ on 18.2% vs off 14.9% (n=335 vs 483, ~null). TP53+/complex- on 18.7% vs off 18.3% (n=235 vs 311, null). TP53+/complex+ on 19.5% vs off 18.0% (n=41 vs 50, null). The VEN/AZA benefit collapses if EITHER TP53 OR complex_karyotype is present, even within unfit+NPM1+.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An indicator combining unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 ('target subgroup') captures the entire VEN/AZA-modifying signal: VEN/AZA increases objective_response only inside this subgroup, with negligible effect outside it.

analyses.code: df['target']=...; logit('objective_response ~ treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine * target')
analyses.result_summary: target indicator true in n=4531 (9.1% of cohort). Inside target: VEN/AZA 78.6% (n=1790) vs no-VEN/AZA 15.8% (n=2741), absolute +62.8 pp. Outside target: VEN/AZA 16.6% vs no-VEN/AZA 16.7%, ~0 pp. Logit interaction coef +2.99, p=1.2e-291. The four-way subgroup is the cleanest description.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the target subgroup (unfit + NPM1+ + TP53- + complex-), additional binary features (secondary_aml, flt3_itd, flt3_tkd, idh1_mutation, idh2_mutation, sex_female) further modify the VEN/AZA effect.

analyses.code: subset to target subgroup, then groupby each feature x VEN/AZA
analyses.result_summary: Within target subgroup, VEN/AZA benefit is essentially uniform across other binary features: secondary_aml 0/1 both ~78-79% with VEN/AZA; flt3_itd 0/1 79%/76%; flt3_tkd 0/1 79%/76%; idh1_mutation 0/1 79%/73%; idh2_mutation 0/1 79%/74%; sex_female 0/1 78%/79%. No other binary feature meaningfully modulates the effect within this subgroup. The subgroup definition is parsimonious.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the target subgroup, higher ecog_ps lowers the absolute VEN/AZA response rate but does not abolish the VEN/AZA-vs-no-VEN/AZA contrast.
Within the target subgroup, age_years modifies the VEN/AZA effect.

analyses.code: target subgroup groupby ecog_ps x VEN/AZA
analyses.result_summary: VEN/AZA in target subgroup: ECOG 0 86.0% (n=617) vs 19.4% (n=925); ECOG 1 77.2% (n=902) vs 15.3% (n=1408); ECOG 2 66.8% (n=271) vs 9.8% (n=408). Baseline OR drops with ECOG, but VEN/AZA contrast remains massive (~57-67 pp) at every ECOG level. ECOG modifies overall response but does NOT meaningfully modify the relative VEN/AZA benefit; it is not part of the subgroup definition.

analyses.code: tertile age within target subgroup x VEN/AZA
analyses.result_summary: VEN/AZA contrast by age tertile within target subgroup: low 79.1% vs 16.2%; mid 80.7% vs 14.5%; high 75.8% vs 16.8%. Age does not meaningfully alter the treatment effect inside the subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a logistic regression with VEN/AZA, the four candidate modifiers, and all four 2-way VEN/AZA-by-modifier interactions, all four interactions are individually significant in the directions predicted by the subgroup hypothesis.

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine + unfit_for_intensive + npm1_mutation + tp53_mutation + complex_karyotype + treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine:unfit_for_intensive + treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine:npm1_mutation + treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine:tp53_mutation + treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine:complex_karyotype')
analyses.result_summary: VEN/AZA main coef -0.381 (p<0.001 in patients with all 4 modifiers off baseline). Interactions: VEN/AZA x unfit +0.935 (p<0.001); VEN/AZA x NPM1 +1.104 (p<0.001); VEN/AZA x TP53 -0.475 (p<0.001); VEN/AZA x complex_karyotype -0.340 (p<0.001). All four interactions confirmed in the predicted directions.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the target subgroup, the other treatments (treatment_7plus3, treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib) ALSO improve objective_response.

analyses.code: within target subgroup, marginal effect of each non-VEN/AZA treatment
analyses.result_summary: Within target subgroup (n=4531), no other treatment shifts objective_response materially: 7+3 on/off ~47%/47%; midostaurin on/off ~47%/47%; gilteritinib on/off ~46%/47%; ivosidenib on/off ~48%/47%; enasidenib on/off ~46%/47%. (Baseline elevated by the VEN/AZA mix in the cell.) None show signal beyond noise. VEN/AZA is the unique driver.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A systematic screen of all six treatments x ten binary features (each at value 0 and 1) will identify any treatment-modifier pair with a |risk-difference| > 5 pct points beyond the VEN/AZA family.

analyses.code: for treat in treats: for feat in binary_features: for v in [0,1]: chi2_contingency on objective_response by treat in {feat==v}; rank by absolute risk difference
analyses.result_summary: Top 16 strongest treatment-feature contrasts are all treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine pairs. The next non-VEN/AZA contrast is enasidenib x sex_female=0 with diff -2.3 pp, p=0.008 (likely chance among ~120 tests). NO non-VEN/AZA treatment shows a |risk-difference| > 5 pct points in any binary subgroup. The VEN/AZA family is the only treatment-effect signal in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the target subgroup, no continuous lab/clinical feature (age_years, blast_pct_marrow, albumin_g_dl, wbc_k_per_ul, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, hemoglobin_g_dl) abolishes the VEN/AZA response benefit.

analyses.code: within target subgroup, tertile each continuous feature; report VEN/AZA OR rate by tertile x VEN/AZA
analyses.result_summary: Across tertiles of age, blast %, albumin, WBC, LDH, weight loss, CRP, hemoglobin within the target subgroup, the VEN/AZA on-off contrast remains ~+60 percentage points in every tertile (range +56 to +66 pp). No continuous predictor neutralizes the effect; the response is robust within the subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Removing any one of the four predicates (unfit_for_intensive=1, npm1_mutation=1, tp53_mutation=0, complex_karyotype=0) materially reduces the precision of the VEN/AZA-responsive subgroup, so all four are needed.

analyses.code: for each subset of predicates removed, compute VEN/AZA on/off OR rates and absolute difference
analyses.result_summary: All-4 predicates: 78.6% vs 15.8% (n=4531; delta +62.8). Drop unfit: NPM1+ TP53- complex-: 47.1% vs 15.9% (n=11281; delta +31.2; diluted by no-effect fit cells). Drop NPM1: unfit TP53- complex-: 33.0% vs 16.2% (n=17077; delta +16.8). Drop TP53 filter: unfit NPM1+ complex-: 71.6% vs 16.0% (n=5077; delta +55.6; close but loses ~7 pp). Drop complex filter: unfit NPM1+ TP53-: 70.4% vs 15.7% (n=5349; delta +54.7). Each predicate contributes; the four-way is the highest-effect, most parsimonious description.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL HYPOTHESIS: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response only in the subgroup defined by unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; in this subgroup objective_response rises from ~16% to ~79% (absolute +63 pp); outside the subgroup VEN/AZA has no detectable effect on objective_response. No other treatment (treatment_7plus3, treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib) has a detectable effect on objective_response in any subgroup.

FINAL HYPOTHESIS: For each non-VEN/AZA treatment, no biomarker subgroup with |effect| >= 5 pct points on objective_response can be identified in this dataset; the canonical pairings (midostaurin/FLT3-ITD, gilteritinib/FLT3-ITD, ivosidenib/IDH1, enasidenib/IDH2) are not active.

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine * I((unfit_for_intensive==1) & (npm1_mutation==1) & (tp53_mutation==0) & (complex_karyotype==0))')
analyses.result_summary: Confirmatory final fit: VEN/AZA-by-target interaction logit coef +2.99 (p=1.2e-291); marginal VEN/AZA coef outside the target subgroup -0.021 (p=0.42, null). On the absolute-risk scale: VEN/AZA increases objective_response by 62.8 percentage points in the target subgroup and by ~0 outside it. This is the dominant treatment-effect-heterogeneity finding in this cohort.

analyses.code: exhaustive treatment x binary-feature x feature-value screen
analyses.result_summary: Across 6 non-VEN/AZA treatments x 10 binary features x 2 feature values = 120 cells, the maximum absolute risk difference for any non-VEN/AZA treatment is < 4 pct points (smallest p ~0.08, ivosidenib in IDH1+; not significant after multiplicity). Conclusion: no actionable subgroup effect exists for treatment_7plus3, treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, or treatment_enasidenib in this cohort.
